sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Protect one running invariant instead of restarting work from scratch.
class Solution:
    def lengthOfLongestSubstring(self, s: str) -> int:
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Maintaining window state, knowing when to shrink, and separating the invariant from the bookkeeping.
        charSet = set()
        l = 0
        res = 0

        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for r in range(len(s)):
            while s[r] in charSet:
                charSet.remove(s[l])
                l += 1
            charSet.add(s[r])
            res = max(res, r - l + 1)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return res

sources.reviewedCode:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: O(1)

# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Protect one running invariant instead of restarting work from scratch.
class Solution(object):
    def lengthOfLongestSubstring(self, s):
        """
        :type s: str
        :rtype: int
        """
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Maintaining window state, knowing when to shrink, and separating the invariant from the bookkeeping.
        result, left = 0, 0
        lookup = {}
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for right in xrange(len(s)):
            # [Reader note] Decision point
            # Pay attention here: The idea is clean, but it is easy to get the left boundary updates slightly wrong.
            if s[right] in lookup:
                left = max(left, lookup[s[right]]+1)
            lookup[s[right]] = right
            result = max(result, right-left+1)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return result

sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Protect one running invariant instead of restarting work from scratch.
class Solution:
    def lengthOfLongestSubstring(self, s: str) -> int:
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Maintaining window state, knowing when to shrink, and separating the invariant from the bookkeeping.
        cnt = Counter()
        ans = l = 0
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for r, c in enumerate(s):
            cnt[c] += 1
            while cnt[c] > 1:
                cnt[s[l]] -= 1
                l += 1
            ans = max(ans, r - l + 1)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return ans

writeups.title: 003.Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
writeups.excerpt: ### 003.Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters #### 解法1： 双指针的基础题。用```vector<int>Map(256,0)```来模拟字符的Hash表。 如果Map[s[j]]==0，移动右指针将Map[s[j]]=1； 否则移动左指针，将Map[s[i]]=0。 #### 解法2： 对于上述的方法，左指针的前移其实可以跳跃，以加快效率。 对于Map[s[j]]已经有记录的情况，此时左指针可以调到Map[s[j]]+1的地方。但是注意，这个地方可能还不及i本身，所以最终左指针的跳跃应该是```max(i,Map[s[j]]+1)``` [Leetcode Link](https://leetcode.com/problems/longest-substring-without-repeating-characters)
